claim: Albert Einstein was born in Germany and won the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1921 for his work on the photoelectric effect.
page_content:
Albert Einstein (14 March 1879 - 18 April 1955) was a German-born theoretical physicist who is widely held to be one of the greatest and most influential scientists of all time. He is best known for developing the theory of relativity.

FieldValue
Born14 March 1879, Ulm, Kingdom of Wurttemberg, German Empire
Died18 April 1955, Princeton, New Jersey, USA
NationalityGerman (1879-1896), Swiss (1901-1955), American (1940-1955)
FieldPhysics

Einstein received the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1921 for his discovery of the law of the photoelectric effect, a pivotal step in the development of quantum theory.

claim: The Amazon River is the longest river in South America and flows through Brazil, Peru, and Colombia.
page_content:
The Amazon River is the largest river by discharge volume of water in the world, and the disputed longest river system in the world compared to the Nile.

AttributeDetails
Length6,400 km (3,976 mi)
Basin area7,000,000 km2
Discharge209,000 m3/s

The Amazon basin covers parts of several countries:

  • Brazil (60%)
  • Peru (13%)
  • Colombia (10%)
  • Bolivia
  • Ecuador
  • Venezuela
  • Guyana

It is the longest river in South America and second longest in the world after the Nile, although some measurements place it as the longest.

claim: Marie Curie was the first person to win Nobel Prizes in two different scientific disciplines.
page_content:
Marie Sklodowska Curie (7 November 1867 - 4 July 1934) was a Polish and naturalized-French physicist and chemist who conducted pioneering research on radioactivity.

AwardYearFieldShared With
Nobel Prize1903PhysicsPierre Curie, Henri Becquerel
Nobel Prize1911ChemistrySole recipient

She was the first woman to win a Nobel Prize, the first person and the only woman to win the Nobel Prize twice, and the only person to win the Nobel Prize in two scientific fields.

claim: The Great Wall of China was built entirely during the Ming Dynasty and is visible from space.
page_content:
The Great Wall of China is a series of fortifications that were built across the historical northern borders of ancient Chinese states and Imperial China as protection against various nomadic groups.

DynastyPeriodWall Length Built
Qin Dynasty221-206 BC5,000 km
Han Dynasty206 BC - 220 AD10,000 km
Ming Dynasty1368-16448,850 km

The wall was built over many centuries by various dynasties, beginning as early as the 7th century BC. The most well-known sections were built by the Ming Dynasty (1368-1644).

Contrary to popular belief, the Great Wall is not visible from space with the unaided eye. This has been confirmed by multiple astronauts.

claim: Shakespeare wrote exactly 37 plays and was born in London, England.
page_content:
William Shakespeare (26 April 1564 - 23 April 1616) was an English playwright, poet, and actor. He is widely regarded as the greatest writer in the English language and the world's greatest dramatist.

FieldValue
Born26 April 1564, Stratford-upon-Avon, England
Died23 April 1616, Stratford-upon-Avon, England
OccupationPlaywright, poet, actor

Shakespeare's works consist of approximately 39 plays, 154 sonnets, three long narrative poems, and a few other verses. His plays have been translated into every major living language.

  • Comedies: 17 plays
  • Histories: 10 plays
  • Tragedies: 10 plays
  • Romances: 2 plays

claim: The International Space Station orbits Earth at an altitude of approximately 408 kilometers and has been continuously occupied since November 2000.
page_content:
The International Space Station (ISS) is a modular space station in low Earth orbit. The ISS programme is a multi-national collaborative project involving five participating space agencies: NASA, Roscosmos, JAXA, ESA, and CSA.

ParameterValue
Orbital altitude408 km (254 mi)
Orbital speed7.66 km/s
Orbital period92.68 minutes
Launch date20 November 1998

The station has been continuously occupied since 2 November 2000. It has been visited by astronauts and cosmonauts from 19 different countries.

claim: The Mona Lisa was painted by Leonardo da Vinci and is currently housed in the British Museum in London.
page_content:
The Mona Lisa is a half-length portrait painting by Italian artist Leonardo da Vinci. Considered an archetypal masterpiece of the Italian Renaissance, it has been described as the best known, the most visited, the most written about, the most sung about, and the most parodied work of art in the world.

DetailInformation
ArtistLeonardo da Vinci
Datec. 1503-1519
MediumOil on poplar panel
Dimensions77 cm x 53 cm
LocationLouvre Museum, Paris, France

The painting has been on permanent display at the Louvre in Paris since 1797. It was believed to have been painted between 1503 and 1519.

claim: The human body contains 206 bones in adulthood and approximately 270 bones at birth.
page_content:
The human skeleton is the internal framework of the human body. It is composed of around 270 bones at birth, which decreases to about 206 bones by adulthood after some bones get fused together.

RegionNumber of Bones (Adult)
Skull22
Vertebral column26
Thorax25
Upper limbs64
Lower limbs62
Other7
  • Infants: approximately 270 bones
  • Adults: 206 bones
  • Bones fuse during growth and development

claim: Mount Kilimanjaro is located in Kenya and is the tallest mountain in Africa at 5,895 meters.
page_content:
Mount Kilimanjaro is a dormant volcano in Tanzania. It is the highest mountain in Africa and the highest single free-standing mountain above sea level in the world.

AttributeValue
Height5,895 m (19,341 ft)
Prominence5,885 m
LocationKilimanjaro Region, Tanzania
RangeEastern Rift mountains
TypeStratovolcano

Kilimanjaro is located near the border with Kenya, but the mountain and its summit are entirely within Tanzanian territory.

claim: The Euro is used as the official currency in all European Union member states.
page_content:
The euro is the official currency of 20 of the 27 member states of the European Union. This group of states is known as the eurozone. The euro was introduced to world financial markets as an accounting currency on 1 January 1999.

StatusCountriesCount
Eurozone membersGermany, France, Italy, Spain, Netherlands, Belgium, Austria, Finland, Ireland, Portugal, Greece, Luxembourg, Slovakia, Slovenia, Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania, Malta, Cyprus, Croatia20
EU non-euroPoland, Sweden, Czech Republic, Denmark, Hungary, Romania, Bulgaria7
  • Denmark has a formal opt-out from the euro
  • Sweden has a de facto opt-out
  • Other non-euro EU states are legally obliged to adopt the euro once they meet criteria
